comment.body:
Everyone’s modelling this as 1973; structurally it’s closer to a noisy replay of 2022: a big relative‑price shock that accelerates diversification, highlights US energy leverage, and redistributes pain to weaker importers in EM Asia 
The tail isn’t ‘end of global growth’ but a world where Gulf barrels are a little less central, US and a few non‑OPEC producers a little more central, and central banks get yet another excuse to stay tight just long enough to keep vol juicy before they cut a few months down the road
comment.raw_body:
Everyone’s modelling this as 1973; structurally it’s closer to a noisy replay of 2022: a big relative‑price shock that accelerates diversification, highlights US energy leverage, and redistributes pain to weaker importers in EM Asia 
The tail isn’t ‘end of global growth’ but a world where Gulf barrels are a little less central, US and a few non‑OPEC producers a little more central, and central banks get yet another excuse to stay tight just long enough to keep vol juicy before they cut a few months down the road
